description: SISFS provides financial assistance to early-stage startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, market entry, and commercialization.
eligibilityCriteria: A startup recognized by DPIIT.
Must have been incorporated not more than 2 years ago at the time of application.
Must have a business idea to develop a product or service with market fit, viable commercialization, and scope of scaling.
Must not have received more than ₹10 lakh of monetary support under any other Central/State Govt scheme.
benefits: Up to ₹20 lakh as a grant for validation of Proof of Concept, prototype development, or product trials.
Up to ₹50 lakh of investment for market entry, commercialization, or scaling up through convertible debentures or debt.
Mentorship and guidance from incubators.
requiredDocuments: Details of the core team and founders
applicationProcess: Apply online on the Startup India Seed Fund portal (seedfund.startupindia.gov.in).
Select up to 3 preferred incubators.
Applications are evaluated by the Incubator Seed Management Committee (ISMC).
Selected startups sign a legal agreement with the incubator for fund disbursement.

sourceInformation.ministry: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)

description: PMEGP is a major credit-linked subsidy scheme aimed at generating self-employment opportunities through establishment of micro-enterprises in the non-farm sector by helping traditional artisans and unemployed youth.
eligibilityCriteria: Any individual above 18 years of age.
Minimum 8th pass for projects costing above ₹10 lakh in manufacturing and above ₹5 lakh in service sector.
Only new projects are considered. Existing units or units that have availed subsidy under other schemes are not eligible.
benefits: Bank finance up to ₹50 lakh for manufacturing sector and ₹20 lakh for service sector.
Margin money subsidy of 15% to 35% of the project cost depending on the area (urban/rural) and category (general/special).
Special category includes SC/ST, OBC, Minorities, Women, Ex-servicemen, Physically handicapped, NER.
requiredDocuments: Category Certificate (if claiming special category subsidy)
applicationProcess: Submit application online through the KVIC portal (kviconline.gov.in).
Applications are screened by a District Level Task Force Committee (DLTFC).
Approved applications are forwarded to Banks for credit decision.
Applicant must undergo Entrepreneurship Development Programme (EDP) training before loan disbursement.

sourceInformation.ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME)

description: Stand-Up India aims to facilitate bank loans to at least one Scheduled Caste (SC) or Scheduled Tribe (ST) borrower and at least one woman borrower per bank branch for setting up a greenfield enterprise.
eligibilityCriteria: SC/ST and/or women entrepreneurs above 18 years of age.
Loans available only for greenfield (first time) projects in manufacturing, services, agri-allied activities, or the trading sector.
In case of non-individual enterprises, at least 51% of the shareholding must be held by either an SC/ST or woman entrepreneur.
Borrower should not be in default to any bank/financial institution.
benefits: Composite loan (inclusive of term loan and working capital) between ₹10 lakh and ₹1 Crore.
Credit Guarantee cover is available through CGSSI.
Loan repayment period of up to 7 years with a maximum moratorium of 18 months.
applicationProcess: Apply directly at the bank branch.
Alternatively, apply online through the Stand-Up India portal (standupmitra.in).
Or apply through the Lead District Manager (LDM).

sourceInformation.ministry: Department of Financial Services, Ministry of Finance

description: PMMY is a scheme launched to provide loans up to ₹10 lakhs to the non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises. These loans are classified as MUDRA loans under Shishu, Kishore, and Tarun categories.
eligibilityCriteria: Any Indian citizen who has a business plan for a non-farm sector income generating activity.
Business should be in manufacturing, processing, trading, or service sector.
Applicant should not be a defaulter to any bank.
benefits: Collateral-free loans for micro and small businesses.
Shishu: Loans up to ₹50,000 (for startups/new businesses).
Kishore: Loans above ₹50,000 and up to ₹5 lakh (for buying equipment/expansion).
Tarun: Loans above ₹5 lakh and up to ₹10 lakh (for established businesses expanding further).
requiredDocuments: Proof of identity/address of the business enterprise
Quotations for machinery/items to be purchased
applicationProcess: Approach any Commercial Bank, RRB, Small Finance Bank, or MFI.
Submit the MUDRA loan application form along with business plan and KYC documents.
Online application is also available through the Udyamimitra portal.

schemeName: Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE)
description: CGTMSE facilitates access to finance for unserved and under-served Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) by providing guarantee cover to lenders, enabling them to provide collateral-free loans.
eligibilityCriteria: New as well as existing Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs).
Manufacturing and service enterprises, including retail trade.
Excludes educational institutions, agriculture, self-help groups (SHGs), and training institutions.
benefits: Collateral-free and third-party guarantee-free loans up to ₹5 Crore.
Guarantee cover ranging from 75% to 85% of the sanctioned amount.
85% guarantee cover for micro-enterprises up to ₹5 lakh, women entrepreneurs, and units in the North East Region.
applicationProcess: The entrepreneur does not apply directly to CGTMSE.
Apply for a collateral-free loan at any Member Lending Institution (Banks/NBFCs).
Once the bank sanctions the loan without collateral, the bank applies to CGTMSE for guarantee cover.

sourceInformation.ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME)

schemeName: Atal Innovation Mission (AIM) - Atal Incubation Centres
description: AIM establishes Atal Incubation Centres (AICs) to nurture innovative startups in their pursuit to become scalable and sustainable enterprises, providing them with world-class facilities.
eligibilityCriteria: Startups working in deep-tech, hardware, software, or social sectors with an innovative product/service.
Must be incorporated as a Private Limited Company, LLP, or Registered Partnership.
Must be accepted into a designated Atal Incubation Centre.
benefits: Access to co-working spaces, labs, and testing facilities at the incubator.
Mentoring from industry experts and academicians.
Seed funding opportunities.
Networking with investors and venture capitalists.
requiredDocuments: Pitch Deck / Business Plan detailing the innovation
applicationProcess: Locate an Atal Incubation Centre (AIC) specializing in the startup's domain.
Apply directly to the AIC during their cohort intake programs.
Undergo screening and pitching sessions before selection.
